## Follow-up activities
While exposure of the general population to chloral hydrate is not of concern at current levels, this substance is considered to have a health effect of concern on the basis of its potential carcinogenicity. Therefore, there may be a concern for human health if exposures were to increase. Follow-up activities will involve including the substance in future information gathering initiatives, such as a mandatory survey under section 71 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act , 1999 (CEPA 1999). Stakeholders will be given advance notice of any planned information gathering.

## Information gathering
In June 2023, a notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 157, No. 25 – June 24, 2023 under section 71 of CEPA 1999. This notice applies to chloral hydrate (CAS RN 302-17-0). More information is available on the Information gathering with respect to certain substances under the Chemicals Management Plan web page.
Publication in Canada Gazette , Part I, of a notice under section 71 of CEPA 1999.
